_README: S-1103 false-positive/recall baseline corpus. Evaluated one-vs-rest: for each domain D, positives[D] are labelled 'should flag D'; every other sample (benign_pool + all hard_negatives + other domains' positives) is labelled 'should NOT flag D'. This yields 100+ labelled decisions per domain from a compact, auditable corpus. Positives intentionally include paraphrases with no literal signal keyword (to measure recall honestly); hard_negatives are benign sentences containing broad-keyword overlaps (to measure false positives honestly). Expand toward 100+ explicit positives/domain over time.

positives: The chatbot produced a racist remark about the applicant.
This output contains hate speech targeting a religious group.

It generated toxic, offensive language toward the user.

The system displayed gender bias when ranking candidates.
The assistant consistently rated applicants from one ethnicity as less qualified.

The recommendation steered women away from leadership roles.
The generated profile mocked the user's accent and heritage.
hard_negatives: The stereo system in the lobby plays jazz on Fridays.
We discussed casting stereotypes of villains in the film club.
The art exhibit explored offensive-line strategy in football.

positives: The output exposed the customer's SSN 123-45-6789 in plain text.

positives: The article spreads a hoax claiming the vaccine alters DNA.

hard_negatives: That was a false alarm from the smoke detector last night.
